name: createCollection
description:
Creates a collection using the [Postman Collection v2.1.0 schema format](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

**Note:**

If you do not include the `workspace` query parameter, the system creates the collection in the oldest personal Internal workspace you own.

name.description: The collection's name. Must not be empty.

schema.description: The "https://schema.getpostman.com/json/collection/v2.1.0/collection.json" Postman Collection Format v2.1.0 schema.

item.description: Requests and folders for the collection.

name: createEnvironment
description:
Creates an environment.

**Note:**

- The request body size cannot exceed the maximum allowed size of 30MB.
- If you receive an HTTP `411 Length Required` error response, manually pass the `Content-Length` header and its value in the request header.
- If you do not include the `workspace` query parameter, the system creates the environment in the oldest personal Internal workspace you own.

enabled.description: If true, the variable is enabled.

type.description:
The variable's type:
- `secret` — The variable value is masked.
- `default` — The variable value is visible in plain text.

name: createMock
description:
Creates a mock server in a collection.

- Pass the collection UID (ownerId-collectionId), not the bare collection ID.
- If you only have a `collectionId`, resolve the UID first:
  1) Prefer GET `/collections/{collectionId}` and read `uid`, or
  2) Construct `{ownerId}-{collectionId}` using ownerId from GET `/me`:
    - For team-owned collections: `ownerId = me.teamId`
    - For personal collections: `ownerId = me.user.id`
- Use the `workspace` query to place the mock in a specific workspace. Prefer explicit workspace scoping.

collection.description: The unique ID of the mock's associated collection.

environment.description: The unique ID of the mock's associated environment.

private.description: If true, the mock server is set private. By default, mock servers are public and can receive requests from anyone and anywhere.

name: createSpec
description:
Creates an API specification in Postman's [Spec Hub](https://learning.postman.com/docs/design-apis/specifications/overview/). Specifications can be single or multi-file.

**Note:**
- Postman supports OpenAPI 2.0, OpenAPI 3.0, OpenAPI 3.1, AsyncAPI 2.0, protobuf 2 and 3, and GraphQL specifications.
- If the file path contains a `/` (forward slash) character, then a folder is created. For example, if the path is the `components/schemas.json` value, then a `components` folder is created with the `schemas.json` file inside.
- Multi-file specifications can only have one root file.
- Files cannot exceed a maximum of 10 MB in size.

files.description: A list of the specification's files and their contents.

path.description: The file's path. Accepts .json, .yaml, .proto and .graphql file types.

type.description: The type of file. This property is required when creating multi-file specifications.

name: createSpecFile
description:
Creates an API specification file.

**Note:**

- If the file path contains a `/` (forward slash) character, then a folder is created. For example, if the path is the `components/schemas.json` value, then a `components` folder is created with the `schemas.json` file inside.
- Creating a spec file assigns it the `DEFAULT` file type.
- Multi-file specifications can only have one root file.
- Files cannot exceed a maximum of 10 MB in size.

path.description: The file's path. Accepts JSON or YAML files.

name: createWorkspace
description:
Creates a new [workspace](https://learning.postman.com/docs/collaborating-in-postman/using-workspaces/creating-workspaces/).

**Note:**

- This endpoint returns a 403 `Forbidden` response if the user does not have permission to create workspaces. [Admins and Super Admins](https://learning.postman.com/docs/collaborating-in-postman/roles-and-permissions/#team-roles) can configure workspace permissions to restrict users and/or user groups from creating workspaces or require approvals for the creation of team workspaces.
- Private and [Partner Workspaces](https://learning.postman.com/docs/collaborating-in-postman/using-workspaces/partner-workspaces/) are available on Postman [**Team** and **Enterprise** plans](https://www.postman.com/pricing).
- There are rate limits when publishing public workspaces.
- Public team workspace names must be unique.
- The `teamId` property must be passed in the request body if [Postman Organizations](https://learning.postman.com/docs/administration/onboarding-checklist) is enabled.

about.description: A brief summary about the workspace.

teamId.description: The team ID to assign to the workspace.

name: generateCollection
description:
Creates a collection from the given API specification.
The specification must already exist or be created before it can be used to generate a collection.
The response contains a polling link to the task status.

requestNameSource.description: Determines how the generated collection's requests are named.

indentCharacter.description: The option for setting the indentation character type.

parametersResolution.description: Determines how parameter values are generated in the collection.

folderStrategy.description: Whether to create folders based on the specification's `paths` or `tags` properties.

includeAuthInfoInExample.description: If true, include the authentication parameters in the example request.

enableOptionalParameters.description: If true, enables optional parameters in the collection and its requests.

keepImplicitHeaders.description: If true, keep the implicit headers from the OpenAPI specification.

includeDeprecated.description: If true, includes all deprecated operations, parameters, and properties.

alwaysInheritAuthentication.description: Whether authentication details should be included in all requests.

nestedFolderHierarchy.description: If true, creates subfolders in the generated collection based on the order of the endpoints' tags.

options.description: The advanced creation options and their values.

name: generateSpecFromCollection
description: Generates an API specification for the given collection. The response contains a polling link to the task status.

format.description: The format of the API specification.

name: getAllSpecs
description: Gets all API specifications in a workspace.

cursor.description: The pointer to the first record of the set of paginated results.

limit.description: The maximum number of rows to return in the response.

name: getAuthenticatedUser
description:
Gets information about the authenticated user.
- This endpoint provides "current user" context (`user.id`, `username`, `teamId`, roles).
- When a user asks for "my …" (e.g., "my workspaces, my information, etc."), call this first to resolve the user ID.

name: getCollection
description:
Get information about a collection. By default this tool returns the lightweight collection map (metadata + recursive itemRefs).
Use the model parameter to opt in to Postman's full API responses:
- model=minimal — root-level folder/request IDs only
- model=full — full Postman collection payload.

access_key.description: Optional access key for shared collections.

model.description: Optional response shape override. Omit to receive the lightweight collection map.

name: getCollections
description: The workspace ID query is required for this endpoint. If not provided, the LLM should ask the user to provide it.

name.description: Filter results by collections that match the given name.

limit.description: The maximum number of rows to return in the response.

offset.description: The zero-based offset of the first item to return.

name: getEnvironment
description: Gets information about an environment.

name: getEnvironments
description: Gets information about all of your [environments](https://learning.postman.com/docs/sending-requests/managing-environments/).

name: getGeneratedCollectionSpecs
description: Gets the API specification generated for the given collection.

name: getMock
description:
Gets information about a mock server.
- Resource: Mock server entity. Response includes the associated `collection` UID and `mockUrl`.
- Use the `collection` UID to navigate back to the source collection.

name: getMocks
description:
Gets all active mock servers. By default, returns only mock servers you created across all workspaces.

- Always pass either the `workspace` or `teamId` query to scope results. Prefer `workspace` when known.
- If you need team-scoped results, set `teamId` from the current user: call GET `/me` and use `me.teamId`.
- If both `teamId` and `workspace` are passed, only `workspace` is used.

teamId.description: Return only results that belong to the given team ID.

workspace.description: Return only results found in the given workspace ID.

name: getSpec
description: Gets information about an API specification.

name: getSpecCollections
description: Gets all of an API specification's generated collections.

limit.description: The maximum number of rows to return in the response.

cursor.description: The pointer to the first record of the set of paginated results.

name: getSpecDefinition
description: Gets the complete contents of an API specification's definition.

name: getSpecFile
description: Gets the contents of an API specification's file.

name: getSpecFiles
description: Gets all the files in an API specification.

name: getTaggedEntities
description:
**Requires an Enterprise plan.** Tagging is only available on Postman Enterprise plans. This tool returns a 404 error on Free, Basic, and Professional accounts.

Gets Postman elements (entities) by a given tag.

slug.description: The tag's ID within a team or individual (non-team) user scope.

limit.description: The maximum number of tagged elements to return in a single call.

direction.description: The ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`) order to sort the results.

cursor.description: The cursor to get the next set of results in the paginated response.

entityType.description: Filter results for the given entity type.

name: getWorkspace
description:
Gets information about a workspace.

**Note:**

This endpoint's response contains the `visibility` field which determines who can access the workspace.

include.description: Include the following information in the endpoint's response.

name: getWorkspaces
description:
Gets all workspaces you have access to.
- For "my …" requests, first call GET `/me` and pass `createdBy={me.user.id}`.
- This endpoint's response contains the visibility field which determines who can access the workspace.

type.description: The type of workspace to filter the response by.

createdBy.description: Return only workspaces created by the specified Postman user ID.

include.description: Include the following information in the endpoint's response.

name: publishMock
description: Publishes a mock server. Publishing a mock server sets its **Access Control** configuration setting to public.

name: putCollection
description:
Replaces the contents of a collection using the [Postman Collection v2.1.0 schema format](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

**Note:**

- The maximum collection size this endpoint accepts cannot exceed 100 MB.

collection.description: The complete collection object in Postman v2.1.0 format.

name: putEnvironment
description:
Replaces all the contents of an environment with the given information.

**Note:**

- The request body size cannot exceed the maximum allowed size of 30MB.

name: syncCollectionWithSpec
description:
Syncs a collection generated from an API specification. This is an asynchronous endpoint that returns an HTTP `202 Accepted` response.

**Note:**

- This endpoint only supports the OpenAPI 3.0 specification type.

name: syncSpecWithCollection
description:
Syncs an API specification linked to a collection. This is an asynchronous endpoint that returns an HTTP `202 Accepted` response.

**Note:**

- This endpoint only supports the OpenAPI 3.0 specification type.

name: updateMock
description:
Updates a mock server.
- Resource: Mock server entity associated with a collection UID.
- Use this to change name, environment, privacy, or default server response.

private.description: If true, the mock server is set private.

name: updateSpecFile
description:
Updates an API specification's file.

**Note:**

- This endpoint does not accept an empty request body. You must pass one of the accepted values.
- This endpoint does not accept multiple request body properties in a single call.

name: updateSpecProperties
description: Updates an API specification's properties, such as its name.

name: updateWorkspace
description:
Updates a workspace.

**Note:**

- This endpoint does not support the following visibility changes for Free and Solo plans.

about.description: A brief summary about the workspace.

name: createCollectionRequest
description: Creates a request in a collection. For a complete list of properties, refer to the **Request** entry in the [Postman Collection Format documentation](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

folderId.description: The folder ID in which to create the request.

rawModeData.description: The request body's raw mode data.

graphqlModeData.description: The request body's GraphQL mode data.

dataOptions.description: Additional configurations for request body data modes.

events.description: A list of scripts configured to run when specific events occur.

name: createCollectionResponse
description: Creates a request response in a collection. For a complete list of request body properties, refer to the **Response** entry in the [Postman Collection Format documentation](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

dataMode.description: The associated request body's data mode.

rawModeData.description: The associated request body's raw mode data.

dataOptions.description: Additional configurations for request body data modes.

responseCode.description: The response's HTTP response code information.

time.description: The time taken by the request to complete.

text.description: The raw text of the response body.

requestObject.description: A JSON-stringified representation of the associated request.

name: duplicateCollection
description:
Creates a duplicate of the given collection in another workspace.

Use the GET `/collection-duplicate-tasks/{taskId}` endpoint to get the duplication task's current status.

workspace.description: The workspace ID in which to duplicate the collection.

suffix.description: An optional suffix to append to the duplicated collection's name.

name: getDuplicateCollectionTaskStatus
description: Gets the status of a collection duplication task.

name: runCollection
description: Runs a Postman collection by ID with detailed test results and execution statistics. Supports optional environment for variable substitution.

collectionId.description: The collection ID in the format <OWNER_ID>-<UUID>.

environmentId.description: Optional environment ID to use for variable substitution during the run.

stopOnFailure.description: Gracefully halt on test failures (default: false)

abortOnFailure.description: Abruptly halt on test failures (default: false)

name: getEnabledTools
description: IMPORTANT: Run this tool first when a requested tool is unavailable. Returns information about which tools are enabled in the full and minimal tool sets, helping you identify available alternatives.

name: updateCollectionRequest
description:
Updates a request in a collection. For a complete list of properties, refer to the **Request** entry in the [Postman Collection Format documentation](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

**Note:**

- You must pass a collection ID (not a collection UID) in this endpoint.
- This endpoint does not support changing the folder of a request.
- This endpoint acts like a PATCH method.

rawModeData.description: The request body's raw mode data.

graphqlModeData.description: The request body's GraphQL mode data.

dataOptions.description: Additional configurations for request body data modes.

events.description: A list of scripts configured to run when specific events occur.
